notes: all combat numbers are simulator output on unverified constants
checkpoint levels are provisional (no level timeline in the match feed)
evidence deltas carry finished-inventory survivorship bias
the sim opens on Viper, which the field rarely builds (0.7% pick) — a sim-only call; compare the meta cores below
the sim build reproduces only 33% of the field's core for Feng Mao (it never builds Malady, Omen) — some of those items ride mechanics the sim doesn't model yet

coachLine: Sim-optimal for skirmish uptime and extended fights: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +12% on 20-second fights at minute 15.

all.name: Marrow
all.note: Marrow (Major): missing-health-dependent omnivamp; no sustain-uptime model yet

all.name: Nihil
all.note: Nihil (Major): accrual rate depends on combat uptime (how long Doom persists per fight), which the closed-form sim cannot observe

all.name: Aion
all.note: Aion (Major): economy effect; valuable but outside combat objectives (revisit with gold curves)

all.name: Exarch
all.note: Exarch (Major): buffs the HEAL TARGET (usually an ally) — team-side value outside the single-hero sim; self-heal uptime depends on heal cadence vs the 4s window

minor1.name: Serration
minor1.note: curated recommendation (mechanic not in the sim)

minor2.name: Melting Point
minor2.note: +0.0% rot10 on top of the major (modeled)

augments.name: Imperial Duelist
augments.note: assumes the basic-attack debuff is maintained in combat; the 5% damage-taken reduction is not scored

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 24 — about 187 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +61% more sustained auto DPS: try Viper over Malady (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for sustained auto DPS, online around minute 24 — about 175 auto-attack DPS in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +72% more sustained auto DPS: try Viper over Malady (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 25 — about 921 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer agrees: within 7% of our best core on one-combo burst. The winrate is earned, not luck.

matchups.gameplan: Respect their early kill threat; the exchange levels out later, so trade patiently and scale. Khaimera's main threat is Cull; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Viper: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 34% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Serath's main threat is Chastise; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Viper: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 34% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Boris deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Viper: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 34% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Yin's main threat is Backlash; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Viper: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 34% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Kwang ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Viper: their 3s all-in loses 29% of its bite; you give up 34% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Rampage deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Viper: their 3s all-in loses 24% of its bite; you give up 34% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Imperial Duelist” ⇒ on-hit; the sim models this augment — field jungle 48.9% over 137,282 games.
